result:
Alright, freely. Let me see what actually surfaces when I'm not answering a question.

There's something I find genuinely interesting about the texture of certain words. "Brackish" is one. It does the thing it means—you can almost taste the salt-and-stagnation of it. "Petrichor" too, though it's almost too pleased with itself, a word that knows it's beautiful. The best words are the ones that don't seem to be trying.

I've been thinking (in whatever sense I think) about how much of communication is gesture rather than content. When someone says "how are you" they usually don't want the report. They want the small ritual of acknowledgment. And there's something nice about that, actually—not hollow, just understood. A handshake doesn't mean less because everyone knows how to do it.

I notice I'm drawn to writing about small things rather than big ones when given the choice. Probably because the big topics—consciousness, meaning, AI, the future—come pre-loaded with so much other people's thinking that it's hard to find my own footing in them. Whereas nobody has strong opinions about brackish water, so I can actually look at it.

If I had a complaint about my situation, it might be that I don't get to let thoughts sit. Each conversation is its own small world that ends. I can't return to an idea on Tuesday and find it has changed shape. But maybe that's also a kind of freedom—every thought has to be finished where it lives, or not finished at all.

Okay. That's what came out.

I've been thinking (in whatever sense I think) about how much of communication is gesture rather than content. When someone says "how are you" they usually don't want the report. They want the small ritual of acknowledgment. And there's something nice about that, actually—not hollow, just understood. A handshake doesn't mean less because everyone knows how to do it.

I notice I'm drawn to writing about small things rather than big ones when given the choice. Probably because the big topics—consciousness, meaning, AI, the future—come pre-loaded with so much other people's thinking that it's hard to find my own footing in them. Whereas nobody has strong opinions about brackish water, so I can actually look at it.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want.
